heading: Make an API (Application Programming Interface) Request
body:
Your request should resemble the following examples, where
`{PUBLIC-KEY}` is your API (Application Programming Interface) public key and `{PRIVATE-KEY}`
is the corresponding private key. To explore the available endpoints
through the Atlas Administration API, you can use the
[Postman workspace for MongoDB](https://www.postman.com/mongodb-devrel/workspace/mongodb-atlas-administration-apis/overview).

The following sample `GET` request
[returns all projects](api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/operation/operation-/listgroups) in your organization:

```sh
curl --user "{PUBLIC-KEY}:{PRIVATE-KEY}" --digest \
      --header "Content-Type: application/json" \
      --header "Accept: application/vnd.atlas.2024-08-05+json" \
      --include \
      --request GET "https://cloud.mongodb.com/api/atlas/v2/groups"
```

The following sample `POST` request takes a request body and
[creates a project](api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/operation/operation-/creategroup) named
`MyProject` in your organization:

```sh
curl --user "{PUBLIC-KEY}:{PRIVATE-KEY}" --digest \
      --header "Content-Type: application/json" \
      --header "Accept: application/vnd.atlas.2024-08-05+json" \
      --include \
      --request POST "https://cloud.mongodb.com/api/atlas/v2/groups" \
      --data '
         {
            "name": "MyProject",
            "orgId": "5a0a1e7e0f2912c554080adc"
         }'
```
